Traffic Light Controller
  • top
  • pwm_controller
  • pwm_controller — Entity

pwm_controller — Entity

Source file: src/pwm_controller.sv.

Annotated Source

src/pwm_controller.sv
  1// PWM controller module.
  2//
  3// Generates a variable duty-cycle PWM signal using a 3-state FSM:
  4// IDLE -> COUNTING -> DONE. The duty cycle is set via the duty_i port.
  5// The period is fixed at 256 clock cycles.
  6module pwm_controller #(
  7    parameter int WIDTH = 8
  8)(
  9    // System clock, rising-edge triggered.
 10    input  logic              clk,
 11    // Synchronous active-high reset.
 12    input  logic              rst,
 13    // Enable signal. FSM runs when asserted.
 14    input  logic              en,
 15    // Duty cycle: 0=0%, 255=100%.
 16    input  logic [WIDTH-1:0]  duty_i,
 17    // PWM output signal.
 18    output logic              pwm_o,
 19    // Active high when one full PWM period has completed.
 20    output logic              period_done
 21);
 22
 23    // FSM state encoding
 24    typedef enum logic [1:0] {
 25        // Waiting for enable.
 26        IDLE,
 27        // Counting through the PWM period.
 28        COUNTING,
 29        // Period complete, pulse period_done for one cycle.
 30        DONE
 31    } t_state;
 32
 33    t_state state, next_state;
 34
 35    // Period counter register.
 36    logic [WIDTH-1:0] count;
 37
 38    // p_state_reg: Clocked state and counter register.
 39    //
 40    // Captures next_state on each rising edge of clk.
 41    // Synchronous reset drives state to IDLE and clears counter.
 42    // Counter increments each cycle in COUNTING state.
 43    //
 44    // // wavedrom::
 45    //
 46    //    { "signal": [
 47    //      { "name": "clk",   "wave": "P......." },
 48    //      { "name": "rst",   "wave": "1.0....." },
 49    //      { "name": "en",    "wave": "0......." },
 50    //      { "name": "state", "wave": "=.=.....", "data": ["(any)","IDLE"] },
 51    //      { "name": "pwm_o", "wave": "x.0....." }
 52    //    ]}
 53    always_ff @(posedge clk) begin : p_state_reg
 54        if (rst) begin
 55            state <= IDLE;
 56            count <= '0;
 57        end else begin
 58            state <= next_state;
 59            if (state == COUNTING)
 60                count <= count + 1'b1;
 61            else
 62                count <= '0;
 63        end
 64    end
 65
 66    // p_next_state: Combinational next-state logic.
 67    //
 68    // FSM advances only when en is asserted.
 69    // COUNTING state runs for a full 256-cycle period then moves to DONE.
 70    //
 71    // Transitions:
 72    //   IDLE     + en           -> COUNTING
 73    //   COUNTING + count==255   -> DONE
 74    //   DONE                    -> IDLE
 75    //
 76    // // wavedrom::
 77    //
 78    //    { "signal": [
 79    //      { "name": "clk",         "wave": "P............." },
 80    //      { "name": "en",          "wave": "01...........0" },
 81    //      { "name": "state",       "wave": "=.=........=..", "data": ["IDLE","COUNTING","DONE"] },
 82    //      { "name": "count",       "wave": "=.=........=..", "data": ["0","1..255","0"] },
 83    //      { "name": "period_done", "wave": "0..........1.0" }
 84    //    ]}
 85    always_comb begin : p_next_state
 86        next_state = state;
 87        case (state)
 88            IDLE:     if (en)              next_state = COUNTING;
 89            COUNTING: if (count == 8'hFF)  next_state = DONE;
 90            DONE:                          next_state = IDLE;
 91            default:                       next_state = IDLE;
 92        endcase
 93    end
 94
 95    // p_outputs: Combinational Moore output decode.
 96    //
 97    // PWM output is high when counter is below duty_i threshold.
 98    // period_done pulses for exactly one cycle when state is DONE.
 99    //
100    // Output truth table:
101    //   IDLE     -> pwm_o=0 period_done=0
102    //   COUNTING -> pwm_o=(count < duty_i) period_done=0
103    //   DONE     -> pwm_o=0 period_done=1
104    //
105    // // wavedrom::
106    //
107    //    { "signal": [
108    //      { "name": "state",       "wave": "=.=.=.",     "data": ["IDLE","COUNTING","DONE"] },
109    //      { "name": "count",       "wave": "=.=.=.",     "data": ["x","0..N","N"] },
110    //      { "name": "duty_i",      "wave": "=.....",     "data": ["D"] },
111    //      { "name": "pwm_o",       "wave": "0.x.0." },
112    //      { "name": "period_done", "wave": "0...01" }
113    //    ]}
114    always_comb begin : p_outputs
115        pwm_o       = 1'b0;
116        period_done = 1'b0;
117        case (state)
118            IDLE:     begin pwm_o = 1'b0;           period_done = 1'b0; end
119            COUNTING: begin pwm_o = (count < duty_i); period_done = 1'b0; end
120            DONE:     begin pwm_o = 1'b0;           period_done = 1'b1; end
121            default:  begin pwm_o = 1'b0;           period_done = 1'b0; end
122        endcase
123    end
124
125endmodule
